title: About the Space
subtitle: A Place Set Apart for Prayer
description: The Prayer Space at Redemption is a room set apart for encountering God. Constructed entirely from Douglas fir, the space was designed to slow you down—a quiet, handcrafted room where the walls themselves hold the prayers of others. Just off the main community space at The Commons, a short sloping walkway rises gently into the Prayer Space, serving as a threshold between the activity of daily life and the stillness of prayer.

sections.heading: What to Expect
sections.content: The Prayer Space is calm, quiet, and unhurried. The walls are made of Douglas fir members with slender gaps between them—these gaps are where you tuck your written prayers. You’ll find benches built from the same wood, and the whole room is designed for stillness. There’s no program or schedule to follow. Come as you are, stay as long as you’d like, and pray however feels natural.

sections.heading: How to Get There
sections.content: From the main community space at The Commons, step into the Prayer Space lobby and follow the short walkway that slopes gently upward into the room. The walk itself is part of the experience—a threshold that helps you slow down and shift from the noise of the day into a posture of prayer. Please silence your phone or switch to Do Not Disturb before entering.

sections.heading: The Prayer Walk
sections.content: Before entering the Prayer Space, consider starting with the outdoor Prayer Walk. It begins just outside the northeast exit of The Commons—opposite the Prayer Space—and follows a path under the canopy of palo verde trees, skirting around the Commons lawn. Six stations along the way offer Scripture and a prayer prompt to help you slow down. The walk ends at the Prayer Space, so you can continue your time with God inside.

sections.heading: Hours & Availability
sections.content: The Prayer Space is open whenever The Commons is open—Sunday 7am–12:30pm, Monday through Friday 7am–2pm, Saturday closed. The space is available for individual prayer and is occasionally reserved for small group prayer gatherings.
